subject: Gaming
title: Nintendo Saves the Industry
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Nintendo Saves the Industry.

hook.text: In 1985, American retailers refused to stock video games. Nintendo disguised the NES as a toy.

buildup.text: The Nintendo Seal of Quality ensured only approved games could be sold. Quality control was the key fix.

discovery.text: Super Mario Bros. shipped with every NES. It was so good that people bought the console just to play it.

twist.text: Nintendo controlled 90% of the American console market by 1988. One company single-handedly revived gaming.

climax.text: The NES sold 62 million units worldwide and turned Nintendo into a household name.

punchline.text: They called it a toy to trick stores. It revived an industry.

quiz.question: How did Nintendo get the NES into American stores?
options: They marketed it as a toy, not a video game console
They offered free consoles to retailers
